items.title: U.S. government accepts Qatar's gift of $400M luxury jet to use as Air Force One
items.description: The United States on Wednesday formally accepted a luxury Boeing 747-8 jet valued $400 million from Qatar to convert as Air Force One, the Defense Department confirmed Wednesday.Afterward, President...

items.title: Pentagon accepts luxury Boeing 747 from Qatar for Trump’s use despite backlash
items.description: Pentagon accepts luxury Boeing 747 from Qatar for Trump’s use despite backlash

items.title: The U.S., Trump Accept Qatari Gift to Use As Air Force One. Boeing Stock Is Falling.
items.description: The Pentagon said Wednesday that it had accepted a Qatari  Boeing  747 as a gift that would be used as Air Force One.  “The secretary of defense has accepted a Boeing 747 from Qatar in accordance with all federal rules and regulations,” chief Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell told Barron’s in an emailed statement.  “The Department of Defense will work to ensure proper security measures and functional-mission requirements are considered for an aircraft used to transport the President of the United States.”

items.title: Pentagon Says It Has Taken Possession of 747 From Qatar
items.description: The decision to accept a donated commercial aircraft, which President Trump has said will serve as Air Force One, has raised security and ethics questions.

items.title: Bernstein Maintains Outperform Rating on Boeing (BA), Lifts PT
items.description: On May 20, Bernstein lifted the price target on The Boeing Company (NYSE:BA) stock to $249 from $218 and maintained an Outperform rating. Analyst Douglas Harned raised the price target following the company’s recent developments. The major move was China resuming airline delivery of Boeing planes. The analyst mentioned that Boeing has already received new […]

items.title: Trump's Former VP Mike Pence Calls Qatari Royal Family's Boeing Jet Gift A 'Bad Idea,' Urges President To Reject $400 Million 'Flying Palace'
items.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence is urging President Donald Trump to reject a controversial $400 million Boeing Co. (NYSE:BA) 747-8 jumbo jet gift from Qatar’s royal family, citing national security risks and ethical concerns over what would be the largest foreign gift ever received by the U.S. government. What Happened: Pence criticized the arrangement during Sunday’s NBC interview, stating “Qatar has a long history of playing both sides” and arguing the gift is “inconsistent with our security,

items.title: Boeing Stock Price Targets Are Rising. Where Shares Can Go Now.
items.description: It has taken a while to get to this point, but investors and Wall Street are feeling better about  Boeing  shares.  After an epic rally, the question is, what comes next for Boeing stock?  Coming into Wednesday trading, Boeing stock has climbed almost 50% from its postelection lows of less than $140 a share.
